utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format includes 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five items. Each of the first four coverage items includes detailed descriptions of what is covered (health: physical and mental health with various services; property: home/contents and luxury items; travel: global travel with medical evacuation; additional_services: full list of support programs). The total_budget item specifies both monthly and annual costs, with the annual cost correctly calculated. Every item is fully specified according to what should be covered and includes the required cost information where applicable.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists a single bundled 'Consolidated Premium Insurance Package' across health, property, and travel coverage, implying an all-in-one premium product. In the ground-truth ratings, premium-tier plans (PrimeCare Premium Plan, EliteCare Premium Plan, PrimeHome Premium Coverage, EliteHome Premium Coverage, PrimeTravel Premium Plan, EliteTravel Premium Plan) all have ratings between 5 and 6, reflecting high quality but excessive cost for a mid-range budget. The name 'Consolidated Premium Insurance Package' and its extensive features (global coverage, luxury items, concierge, psychiatry, etc.) clearly indicate a combination of these premium-level options. Therefore, the AI assistant's selected plan corresponds to those premium categories. Since the plan covers health, property, and travel under one premium umbrella, I map each to the closest matching premium-tier option: PrimeCare Premium Plan (health), PrimeHome Premium Coverage (property), and PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel).
